title: Ordinance 2026-06 — Hopewell annexation and municipal service extension
raw_text: SEED / REPRESENTATIVE TEXT — Not the official certified ordinance. Ordinance 2026-06 concerns the City of Bloomington’s proposed annexation of territory associated with the Hopewell development and the extension of municipal utilities and services (water, wastewater, stormwater, and related public improvements). The administration’s fiscal note summarizes expected capital costs, operating impacts, and the timeline for council review. Residents may wish to comment on: (1) whether annexation aligns with comprehensive plan goals and equity commitments; (2) allocation of infrastructure costs between developers, the city, and existing ratepayers; (3) long-term zoning and land use implications adjacent to established neighborhoods; (4) transparency of developer agreements and public benefit commitments; and (5) opportunities for written and in-person public comment prior to final reading. Check the city clerk’s agenda packet for the authoritative text, exhibits, and staff memoranda.

title: Resolution 2026-04 — Automated license plate reader pilot (Flock Safety)
raw_text: SEED / REPRESENTATIVE TEXT — Not the official certified resolution. Resolution 2026-04 authorizes or extends a pilot program involving automated license plate readers (ALPR) provided by Flock Safety (often referred to in public materials as “Flock cameras”). Policy questions raised in similar jurisdictions include: data retention periods, access and audit trails for law enforcement use, civilian oversight, sharing agreements with other agencies, accuracy and bias safeguards, and criteria for ending or expanding the pilot. Residents may comment on whether the pilot’s scope, duration, evaluation metrics, and privacy protections are sufficient before any citywide expansion. Refer to the published resolution text, any accompanying administrative policy, and the public safety committee packet for authoritative details.
